mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during high inflation periods yield positive 5d returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, elevated borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Real rate spreads above 4.0% are followed by positive 5d returns in > 58% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60d returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real personal consumption expenditures decline while real GDP growth remains positive indicates unsustainable inventory accumulation and weak final demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern is followed by positive 20d returns in > 62% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a recessionary exit phase signals improving manufacturing output and economic stabilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 20d returns, with directional accuracy below 50%.
